pests.name: Whitefly
pests.impact: Transmits Leaf Curl Virus (LCV), causing upward curling of leaves, shortened internodes, distortion, stunted plants, and deformed fruits.

pests.name: Aphid
pests.impact: Transmits Papaya Ringspot Virus (PRSV), causing mottled spots on leaves, leaf distortion, fruit deformation, small fruit size, and oily spots on fruit surface in severe infections.

pests.name: Leafhopper
pests.impact: Transmits Phytoplasma disease, causing small, thick, yellowish-green leaves, shortened internodes, stunted plants, chrysanthemum-like appearance, and greening of flowers.

diseases.name: Powdery Mildew
diseases.impact: Initially appears as light yellow spots on mature leaves. These spots enlarge with the growth of white powdery mycelium on the leaf surface. Severely affected leaves turn brown, shrivel, and drop.
diseases.solution: Apply recommended fungicides at the early stage of disease development. Remove infected crop residues after harvest.

diseases.name: Downy Mildew
diseases.impact: Angular yellow spots confined by leaf veins appear on the upper surface of leaves. Leaves become chlorotic, eventually necrotic, and host plant cells die.
diseases.solution: Apply recommended fungicides at the early stage of disease development. Remove infected plant parts after harvest.

diseases.name: Stem Rot
diseases.impact: In the early stages, plants show temporary wilting symptoms. Leaves exhibit yellowing, wilting, and drooping. Eventually, the plant dies. Vascular bundles in the basal region turn yellow or brown. White fungal mycelium can be observed at the base of the plant.
diseases.solution: Destroy infected plants along with the soil. Improve drainage. Apply recommended fungicides at the early stage of disease development.

diseases.name: Gummy Stem Blight
diseases.impact: Lesions typically begin in water-soaked areas on leaves and fruits. Early stages may show chlorotic holes, which then turn light brown and become irregularly shaped. Dark, cracked, sunken spots appear on fruits. Plants may collapse at sunken, bark-cankered areas. Stem lesions often elongate like streaks, and gum exudation may occur from cracks. Dark brown to black pycnidia can be seen on fruits, stems, or leaves.
diseases.solution: Deep plow crop residues immediately after harvest to reduce fungal survival. Apply recommended fungicides at the early stage of disease development.

diseases.name: Bacterial Wilt
diseases.impact: Sudden wilting of leaves is observed. Eventually, the entire plant wilts. Leaves turn brown and dry. Vascular discoloration (browning) occurs. Leads to permanent wilting.
diseases.solution: Destroy infected plants along with the soil. Practice crop rotation with resistant varieties (e.g., Okra, Maize). Implement mixed cropping with Cruciferae family crops. Avoid transferring equipment from infected fields to uninfected fields.

diseases.name: Leaf Curl Virus
diseases.impact: Upward curling of leaves, shortened internodes, and distortion. Results in stunted plants and deformed fruits.

diseases.name: Papaya Ringspot Virus
diseases.impact: Mottled spots on leaves, leaf distortion, fruit deformation, and reduced fruit size. In severe infections, oily spots appear on the fruit surface.

diseases.name: Cucumber Mosaic Virus
diseases.impact: Green mottling and distortion of leaves. Fruits develop green spots, become bright yellow, and decay. Fruit deformation is common.

diseases.name: Zucchini Yellow Mosaic Virus
diseases.impact: Stunted plants, yellowing, occasional chlorosis, mottled and distorted leaves (blisters). Leads to fruit discoloration and deformation.

diseases.name: Phytoplasma Disease
diseases.impact: Small, thick, and yellowish-green leaves. Shortened internodes and stunted plant growth. Chrysanthemum-like appearance. Greening of flowers.
diseases.solution: Remove infected plants. Control vectors (Leafhoppers) using recommended insecticides.
